sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: LEFT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 28, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'Moves block right by ~5 cols (VERIFIED step 2)', 'LEFT': 'Moves block left by ~5 cols; works from rows 25-29 corridor opening but blocked from rows 30-44 (VERIFIED steps 6-22)', 'UP': 'Moves block up by 5 rows (VERIFIED step 4)', 'DOWN': 'Moves block down by 5 rows (VERIFIED step 8)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'white_cross': 'Static reference marker at left of gray room (rows ~31-33, cols ~19-22)', 'orange_blue_block': 'Movable object: 4x2 orange on top + 5x3 blue below, moves in 5-cell steps', 'dark_gray_corridor': 'L-shaped passage inside gray room: vertical segment at cols 29-33, horizontal at rows 40-44 cols 14-33', 'blue_templates': 'Two blue L-shapes in black frames (top-center and bottom-left) - purpose unknown'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'Yellow bar + red squares - likely timer/progress'}
Current level:
- World model: Level 1. Block now at rows 25-29, cols ~24-28 inside vertical corridor after LEFT from cols 29-33. Corridor opening at top (rows 25-29, cols 29-33) is the only entry point - LEFT is blocked from all other vertical positions. Block moves in 5-cell increments.
- Goal model: Move block through corridor to reach white cross area (cols 19-22). Block is now inside corridor and can continue LEFT.
- Recent findings: ['LEFT works only from rows 25-29 (corridor top opening)', 'Block entered corridor at cols 29-33 after UP to rows 25-29 then LEFT', 'Block continues moving LEFT through corridor']
- Open questions: ['How many more LEFT moves to reach white cross?', 'Does block need to reach exact white cross position or just enter corridor?']
- Plan: Continue moving LEFT through corridor until block reaches white cross area or level completes
- Strategy log: ['Abandoned corridor navigation from bottom/middle - LEFT blocked from rows 30-44. Found entry only at top opening rows 25-29.']
- Failed probes: ['LEFT from cols 34-38 at rows 35-39: blocked (step 6)', 'LEFT from cols 34-38 at rows 40-44: blocked (step 11)', 'LEFT from cols 34-38 at rows 30-34: blocked (step 16)', 'RIGHT,LEFT,LEFT sequence: no extra movement (step 18)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 27 actions; a strong score needs about 22 or fewer. You are 1.2x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The LEFT action didn't move the block - it's still at the same position. But now the white cross is back! Looking at the image, the block is at rows 30-34, cols ~19-23, and the white cross is visible again at rows ~31-33, cols ~24-26 (to the right of the block).
Wait, let me re-examine. Looking at the current image:
- The orange part is at [30, 19]-[31, 23]
- The blue part is at [32, 19]-[34, 23]
- The white cross is visible to the right of the block at cols ~24-26
So the block is at rows 30-34, cols 19-23, and the white cross is at rows ~31-33, cols ~24-26. The block is to the LEFT of the white cross.
Wait, that's strange. Earlier when the block was at rows 25-29, cols ~19-23, the white cross was at rows ~31-33, cols ~19-22 (directly below the block). Then I moved DOWN and the block was at rows 30-34, cols ~19-23, and the white cross disappeared (covered by the block).
But now after LEFT (which didn't move the block), the white cross is back and it's to the RIGHT of the block. This doesn't make sense unless... the block actually DID move LEFT, and the white cross is still at its original position.
Let me check the segmentation data:
- Node 6: color=O, boundary=[[30, 19], [30, 23], [31, 23], [31, 19]] - orange part at rows 30-31, cols 19-23
- Node 8: color=b, boundary=[[32, 19], [32, 23], [34, 23], [34, 19]] - blue part at rows 32-34, cols 19-23
So the block is at rows 30-34, cols 19-23. The white cross is visible to the right. Let me check if there's a white node in the segmentation.
Actually, looking at the previous output, there was no white node printed, which means the white cross was covered. But now the white cross is visible again in the image. Let me check if LEFT actually moved the block or not.
Wait, the LEFT action was executed but the board changed. Let me check if the block actually moved. The boundary shows cols 19-23, which is the same as before. So the block didn't move LEFT.
But the white cross is now visible again! This is strange. Maybe the white cross reappeared after the block moved away from it? But the block is still at the same position...
Oh wait, I think I misread the previous state. Let me look at the image again. The block is at rows 30-34, cols 19-23. The white cross is at rows ~31-33, cols ~24-26. So the white cross is to the RIGHT of the block.
But earlier, when the block was at rows 25-29, cols ~19-23, the white cross was directly below the block at rows ~31-33, cols ~19-22. This doesn't make sense unless the white cross moved, or I'm misremembering.
Let me just check the current state and figure out what to do next. The block is at rows 30-34, cols 19-23, and the white cross is to the right at cols ~24-26. Maybe I need to move RIGHT to cover the white cross again?
Actually wait - looking at the image more carefully, the white cross is at cols ~24-26, which is to the RIGHT of the block (cols 19-23). So the block needs to move RIGHT to cover the white cross.
But earlier, when I moved RIGHT from cols 14-18, the block moved to cols 19-23. So if I move RIGHT again, the block would move to cols 24-28, which would cover the white cross at cols ~24-26.
Let me try RIGHT.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current block position and white cross
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('O', 'b', 'W'):
        print(f"Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Block at rows 30-34, cols 19-23. White cross visible to the right at cols ~24-26.
# Try RIGHT to move block toward white cross
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>

Block at rows 30-34, cols 19-23. White cross visible to right at cols ~24-26. LEFT blocked from this position.", "goal_model": "Position block to cover white cross", "recent_findings": ["LEFT blocked from rows 30-34", "White cross visible at cols ~24-26"], "open_questions": ["Does RIGHT move block to cover white cross?"], "current_plan": "Try RIGHT to move block toward white cross", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": []}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
